content:
First note that the condition  
  p(5)+p(25)=1906  
is equivalent (writing p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d) to  
  (125+15625)a + (25+625)b + (5+25)c + 2d =1906  
⇔ 15750 a + 650 b + 30 c +2 d =1906  
⇔ 7875 a + 325 b +15 c + d =953.  

But  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
         =3375a+225b+15c + (953−7875a−325b−15c)  
         =953−4500a−100b  
         =953−100·(45a+b).  

Since a,b are any integers, M=45a+b runs over all ℤ, so  
  p(15)=953−100M.  
To make |p(15)| as small as possible we choose M nearest to 953/100=9.53, i.e. M=10, giving  
  p(15)=953−1000=−47,  
so the minimal possible value of |p(15)| is 47.  

Answer: \boxed{47}.

But  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d  
         =3375a+225b+15c + (953−7875a−325b−15c)

Since a,b are any integers, M=45a+b runs over all ℤ, so  
  p(15)=953−100M.  
To make |p(15)| as small as possible we choose M nearest to 953/100=9.53, i.e. M=10, giving  
  p(15)=953−1000=−47,

Answer: \boxed{47}.
